name: Route of Los Yébenes and the Alg
name_original: Ruta por los campos de Los Yébenes hasta el río Algodor – circular
description: Cycle an easy 12.5-mile circular route through the fields of Los Yebenes to the Algodor River, perfect for touring cyclists.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Los Yébenes offers routes through the Montes de Toledo, a region characterized by a Mediterranean mountain environment. Cyclists will encounter forests of holm oaks, cork oaks, and olive groves, with terrain varying from mostly paved surfaces to unpaved segments. The area features unique geological formations, including quartzite and slate ridges, and the Algodor River, providing diverse landscapes for exploration. Best touring cycling routes around Los Yébenes The most popular touring cycling route is Molinos de Los Yebenes, el Castillo de Orgaz y Sonseca – circular route, a 24.6 miles (39.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 54 minutes to complete. This moderate route passes by historical windmills and offers varied scenery. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Route of Los Yébenes and the Alg, an easy 12.5 miles (20.1 km) path. This route follows the Algodor River through fields, offering a gentle ride. Local touring cyclists also love the Los Yébenes – Los Yébenes Windmills loop from Los Yébenes, a 16.5 miles (26.6 km) trail leading through the local countryside and past the historic windmills, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Touring cycling around Los Yébenes is defined by the Montes de Toledo, holm oak forests, and the Algodor River. The network offers options for easy rides on paved surfaces and more challenging routes with elevation gain. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 50 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Los Yébenes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Los Yébenes?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes around Los Yébenes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult options to explore the region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Los Yébenes?
A: The terrain around Los Yébenes is quite varied, reflecting its location in the Montes de Toledo. You can expect a Mediterranean mountain environment with forests of holm oaks, cork oaks, and olive groves. Routes range from mostly paved surfaces suitable for easier rides to unpaved segments with significant elevation gain for more challenging tours. Unique geological formations like quartzite and slate ridges also contribute to the diverse landscape.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Los Yébenes offers several easy touring cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030406499/route-of-los-yebenes-and-the-alg" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of Los Yébenes and the Alg&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 12.5-mile (20.1 km) path that follows the Algodor River through fields, offering a gentle ride suitable for most skill levels.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many touring cycling routes around Los Yébenes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022691162/molinos-de-los-yebenes-el-castillo-de-orgaz-y-sonseca-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinos de Los Yebenes, el Castillo de Orgaz y Sonseca – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 24.6-mile (39.6 km) trail passing by historical windmills and varied scenery.

Q: What natural features or scenic viewpoints can I discover while cycling?
A: Cycling in Los Yébenes offers views of the Montes de Toledo, characterized by extensive forests of holm oaks, cork oaks, and olive groves. The Algodor River is a prominent feature, and you might encounter unique geological formations known as 'la raña,' reminiscent of an African savanna. Keep an eye out for wildlife such as deer, wild boar, and various birds of prey. For panoramic views, the windmills on the 'Crestería Molinera' are a great spot.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2210456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Counts of Orgaz&lt;/a&gt;, explore the historic windmills on the 'Crestería Molinera' (Mill Ridge), or visit the 16th-century Churches of Santa María and San Juan Bautista in Los Yébenes. The area also has ancient cave paintings and museums like the Natural History Museum and the Hunting and Nature Museum.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Los Yébenes?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Los Yébenes is generally during spring and autumn. These seasons offer milder temperatures, making for more comfortable rides. Summers can be very hot, while winters can be cooler, though often still suitable for cycling on sunny days.

Q: Are there routes that pass by the famous windmills of Los Yébenes?
A: Yes, the windmills are a significant landmark. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134475" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Yébenes – Los Yébenes Windmills loop from Los Yébenes&lt;/a&gt; is a 16.5-mile (26.6 km) trail specifically designed to lead you through the local countryside and past these historic windmills, including the Tío Zacarías Mill which preserves its original machinery.

Q: Are there any options for more challenging or advanced touring cycling routes?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, the Sierra de los Yébenes offers moderate to difficult routes with significant elevation gain. These routes often include unpaved or more technical segments, providing a robust workout amidst the mountainous landscape. An example of a more challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38155603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarrubia de los Ojos – Fuente de Fresno loop from Fuente el Fresno&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 32 miles (52 km) with substantial climbing.

Q: Is Los Yébenes a good destination for touring cyclists looking for diverse landscapes?
A: Los Yébenes is an excellent destination for touring cyclists seeking diverse landscapes. Its position in the Montes de Toledo provides a rich Mediterranean mountain environment, with everything from dense holm oak forests and olive groves to open 'raña' plains and river valleys. The varied terrain and natural features ensure a constantly changing and engaging cycling experience.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Los Yébenes?
A: The komoot community frequently praises the diverse landscapes and the blend of natural beauty with historical points of interest. Cyclists often highlight the peacefulness of the Montes de Toledo, the charm of the Algodor River, and the unique experience of riding past ancient windmills and castles. The variety of routes, from easy riverside paths to challenging mountain climbs, is also a popular aspect.
tips:
Los Yébenes is a fascinating destination in the Montes de Toledo!
Historical and Cultural Heritage
Churches of Santa María and San Juan Bautista: Both from the 16th century, in Mudejar and late Gothic styles.
Guadalerzas Castle: A medieval fortress that still preserves its imposing structure.
Windmills: Located in the "Crestería Molinera" (Molinera Ridge), these windmills stand out for their panoramic views and historical value. The Tío Zacarías Mill preserves its original machinery.
Natural Science Museum: With an impressive blue whale skeleton.
Hunting and Nature Museum: Showcases the hunting tradition of the area.
Hoploteca – Museum of Ancient Weapons: A tour of the evolution of weapons in different contexts.

The Hermitage of the Most Holy Christ of the Veracruz in Los Yébenes (Toledo) is a place of great historical and religious significance, much loved by the local community.
It was built in 1675 by order of the Count of Mora, Don Juan de Távara, as part of a residential complex that included a manor house and a garden.
The hermitage quickly became a center of pilgrimage, thanks to the presence of a miraculous image of the crucified Christ.
It is built of stone and brick, with a single nave and a small bell tower. Its façade is simple and austere, reflecting the sobriety typical of 17th-century religious architecture.
The interior features Baroque decoration, with gilded altarpieces, religious paintings, and sculptures of great artistic value. The main altar, dedicated to the crucified Christ, is the focal point of devotion.
Pretty dry most of the year, the river widens dramatically beyond its bed in early spring

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Route through the fields of Los Yebenes to the Algodor River – circular?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route typically takes around 1 hour and 35 minutes to complete, covering a distance of 20.1 kilometers. This duration is an estimate for an average pace.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is classified as an easy bike ride, making it suitable for cyclists of all fitness levels, including beginners and families. It offers an enjoyable experience without significant technical or physical challenges.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features a varied terrain. You'll encounter approximately 11.1 kilometers of unpaved track, 7.76 kilometers of asphalt, and 1.22 kilometers of paved track. It also includes dedicated trails, roads, and streets, providing a diverse but generally well-maintained riding surface.

faqs.question: Where does the route start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is circular, starting and ending in Los Yébenes. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, as a town, Los Yébenes should offer various parking options for visitors.

faqs.question: What can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll traverse picturesque fields surrounding Los Yébenes, enjoying the natural landscapes of the Montes de Toledo region. The route also passes by the Cristo de la Veracruz and leads to the Río Algodor, crossing it via the Río Algodor Pedestrian Bridge, offering pleasant riverside views.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Montes de Toledo region, where this route is located, experiences a Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential chill of winter.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the Camino Manchego de Santiago (covering about 5% of the route) and the Risco de Los Yébenes trail (covering about 2% of the route).

faqs.question: Is the Route through the fields of Los Yebenes to the Algodor River – circular dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is generally accessible, specific regulations for dogs on this particular path are not provided. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in rural areas where you might encounter wildlife or livestock, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given it's an easy touring bicycle route, essential items include water, snacks, a basic repair kit for your bike, sun protection (sunscreen, hat), and appropriate cycling attire. A phone for navigation and emergencies is also recommended.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The Montes de Toledo region is known for its rich natural environment and Mediterranean flora and fauna. While specific wildlife sightings are not guaranteed, you might observe various bird species and small mammals typical of rural Spanish landscapes.
